<!-- specsmith:generated -- do not edit; regenerated by specsmith sync -->
# Test Specification

## TEST-CM-001. WAL SHA-256 chain is valid after N appends
- **ID:** TEST-CM-001
- **Title:** WAL SHA-256 chain is valid after N appends
- **Description:** Append 10 records to a fresh store. Assert chain_valid() == True. Directly mutate one record's payload in-memory. Assert chain_valid() == False. Reopen store from disk (chain on disk is intact). Assert chain_valid() == True.

- **Requirement ID:** REQ-CM-001
- **Type:** unit
- **Verification Method:** evaluator
- **Confidence:** 1.0

## TEST-CM-002. WAL file is valid NDJSON
- **ID:** TEST-CM-002
- **Title:** WAL file is valid NDJSON
- **Description:** After upsert(), read events.wal. Assert every non-empty line parses as json.loads() without error. Assert no binary magic bytes at file start.

- **Requirement ID:** REQ-CM-002
- **Type:** unit
- **Verification Method:** evaluator
- **Confidence:** 1.0

## TEST-CM-003. ChronoRecord carries all 7 OEA fields
- **ID:** TEST-CM-003
- **Title:** ChronoRecord carries all 7 OEA fields
- **Description:** Create a ChronoRecord with only id and label. Assert to_dict() contains all 7 OEA fields: source_type, confidence, evidence, epistemic_boundary, is_hypothesis, model_assumptions, recursion_depth. Assert all fields survive upsert() + round-trip via WAL replay.

- **Requirement ID:** REQ-CM-003
- **Type:** unit
- **Verification Method:** evaluator
- **Confidence:** 1.0

## TEST-CM-004. Snapshot + WAL tail replay produces correct state
- **ID:** TEST-CM-004
- **Title:** Snapshot + WAL tail replay produces correct state
- **Description:** Write 55 records (triggers snapshot at 50). Close store. Reopen. Assert all 55 records are present and correct. Assert reopened store.wal_seq() == 55.

- **Requirement ID:** REQ-CM-004
- **Type:** unit
- **Verification Method:** evaluator
- **Confidence:** 1.0

## TEST-CM-005. query(rag_filter=True) applies H18 confidence threshold
- **ID:** TEST-CM-005
- **Title:** query(rag_filter=True) applies H18 confidence threshold
- **Description:** Insert records at confidence 0.9, 0.6, 0.5, 0.0. query(rag_filter=True) must return exactly those with confidence >= 0.6 and status == "active". A tombstoned record at confidence 0.9 must not be returned.

- **Requirement ID:** REQ-CM-005
- **Type:** unit
- **Verification Method:** evaluator
- **Confidence:** 1.0

## TEST-CM-006. delete() tombstones record without physical removal
- **ID:** TEST-CM-006
- **Title:** delete() tombstones record without physical removal
- **Description:** Upsert a record. Call delete(record_id). Assert get(record_id) still returns the record (not None). Assert record.status == "tombstone". Assert events.wal contains both the original upsert and the delete event (WAL line count >= 2). Assert query(status="active") does not include the tombstoned record.

- **Requirement ID:** REQ-CM-006
- **Type:** unit
- **Verification Method:** evaluator
- **Confidence:** 1.0

## TEST-CM-007. WAL write is atomic — no partial write on simulated crash
- **ID:** TEST-CM-007
- **Title:** WAL write is atomic — no partial write on simulated crash
- **Description:** Mock os.replace to raise OSError after the tmp write. Assert the original events.wal is unchanged (fallback path may write directly). Assert chain_valid() returns True on the resulting file.

- **Requirement ID:** REQ-CM-007
- **Type:** unit
- **Verification Method:** evaluator
- **Confidence:** 1.0

## TEST-CM-008. migrate_from_json() is idempotent
- **ID:** TEST-CM-008
- **Title:** migrate_from_json() is idempotent
- **Description:** Create a fake .specsmith/ dir with requirements.json and testcases.json. Run migrate_from_json() twice. Assert record count equals the number of unique IDs (no duplicates). Assert all records have source_type="observed". Assert governance status "implemented" maps to ESDB status "active".

- **Requirement ID:** REQ-CM-008
- **Type:** unit
- **Verification Method:** evaluator
- **Confidence:** 1.0

## TEST-CM-009. chronomemory imports without any external package installed
- **ID:** TEST-CM-009
- **Title:** chronomemory imports without any external package installed
- **Description:** Import chronomemory in a subprocess with PYTHONPATH restricted to stdlib + the chronomemory package itself. Assert no ImportError is raised. Assert ChronoStore, ChronoRecord, WalEvent, EsdbBridge are all importable.

- **Requirement ID:** REQ-CM-009
- **Type:** unit
- **Verification Method:** evaluator
- **Confidence:** 1.0

## TEST-CM-010. WAL written by one project is readable by another
- **ID:** TEST-CM-010
- **Title:** WAL written by one project is readable by another
- **Description:** Write a WAL in one temp dir using ChronoStore. Copy events.wal to a second temp dir. Open a new ChronoStore on the second dir. Assert all records are present. Assert chain_valid() == True on the second store.

- **Requirement ID:** REQ-CM-010
- **Type:** integration
- **Verification Method:** evaluator
- **Confidence:** 1.0

## TEST-CM-011. WAL with CRLF line endings parses correctly on all platforms
- **ID:** TEST-CM-011
- **Title:** WAL with CRLF line endings parses correctly on all platforms
- **Description:** Write 3 records. Convert WAL to CRLF line endings. Reopen store. Assert record_count() == 3. This validates cross-platform read compatibility. CI matrix covers ubuntu-latest and windows-latest on Python 3.10–3.13.

- **Requirement ID:** REQ-CM-011
- **Type:** integration
- **Verification Method:** evaluator
- **Confidence:** 1.0

## TEST-CM-012. Graceful recovery from WAL and snapshot corruption
- **ID:** TEST-CM-012
- **Title:** Graceful recovery from WAL and snapshot corruption
- **Description:** 12 corruption scenarios tested: partial last line, truncated at boundary, tampered hash, wrong prev_hash, garbage line, binary bytes, missing hash field, wrong seq type, empty WAL, whitespace-only WAL, null bytes in value, CRLF endings. All must not raise exceptions. 5 snapshot corruption scenarios: truncated JSON, seq higher than WAL, invalid record inside, empty records array, missing snapshot file.

- **Requirement ID:** REQ-CM-012
- **Type:** unit
- **Verification Method:** evaluator
- **Confidence:** 1.0

## TEST-CM-013. Snapshot triggers at seq=50 and tail-replay fills remainder
- **ID:** TEST-CM-013
- **Title:** Snapshot triggers at seq=50 and tail-replay fills remainder
- **Description:** Write 51 records. Assert snapshot.json exists and contains seq==50. Assert record_count() == 51. Assert wal_seq() == 51. Validates that the snapshot+tail architecture works correctly at the snapshot boundary.

- **Requirement ID:** REQ-CM-013
- **Type:** unit
- **Verification Method:** evaluator
- **Confidence:** 1.0

## TEST-CM-014. EsdbBridge delegates to ChronoStore WAL; falls back to JSON
- **ID:** TEST-CM-014
- **Title:** EsdbBridge delegates to ChronoStore WAL; falls back to JSON
- **Description:** With WAL: status().backend contains 'ChronoStore'. requirements() and testcases() return records from the WAL. upsert_record() returns True. Without WAL: status().backend contains 'json'. requirements() returns records from .specsmith/requirements.json. upsert_record() returns False.
- **Requirement ID:** REQ-CM-014
- **Type:** unit
- **Verification Method:** evaluator
- **Confidence:** 1.0

## TEST-CM-015. DepGraph edge operations and WAL persistence
- **ID:** TEST-CM-015
- **Title:** DepGraph edge operations and WAL persistence
- **Description:** 12 tests covering: EDGE_TYPES frozenset has all 9 types; DepGraph starts empty; add_edge stores in memory; invalid edge type raises ValueError; remove_edge deletes (no-op for missing); multiple edges stored; what_depends_on returns correct predecessors; what_contradicts is bidirectional; what_assumes returns outgoing; transitive_successors follows chains; transitive_successors guards circular deps; add_edge with store persists kind=edge WAL record; from_store reloads edges after close+reopen; chain_valid() True after edges.
- **Requirement ID:** REQ-CM-015
- **Type:** unit
- **Verification Method:** evaluator
- **Confidence:** 1.0

## TEST-CM-016. Epistemic rollback cascade and WAL coverage
- **ID:** TEST-CM-016
- **Title:** Epistemic rollback cascade and WAL coverage
- **Description:** 10 tests covering: invalidate sets target status=invalidated; missing record returns report with no direct_change; cascade 1-level (depends_on); cascade 2-level (derived_from chain); confidence halved on cascade; affected_ids includes target + all cascaded; circular dependency terminates; rollback_event WAL record written; chain_valid True after rollback; ChronoStore.invalidate() convenience method delegates correctly.
- **Requirement ID:** REQ-CM-016
- **Type:** unit
- **Verification Method:** evaluator
- **Confidence:** 1.0

## TEST-CM-017. ContextPackCompiler compile, exclusions, and budget enforcement
- **ID:** TEST-CM-017
- **Title:** ContextPackCompiler compile, exclusions, and budget enforcement
- **Description:** 8 tests covering: compile() returns ContextPack instance with correct task_id/goal; includes relevant records matching goal keywords; excludes tombstoned records; excludes invalidated records; excludes low-confidence (< 0.6) records; token_count never exceeds token_budget; entries sorted by confidence desc; to_dict() produces JSON-serializable output with entries and excluded keys.
- **Requirement ID:** REQ-CM-017
- **Type:** unit
- **Verification Method:** evaluator
- **Confidence:** 1.0

## TEST-CM-018. Extended query API - all 18 s23 functions
- **ID:** TEST-CM-018
- **Title:** Extended query API - all 18 s23 functions
- **Description:** 28 tests covering all 18 query functions with >= 2 tests each: what_is_known (confidence filter, kind filter); what_conflicts_with (with/without graph); what_depends_on (with/without graph); what_changed_since (seq=0, high seq); what_requires_reverification (hypothesis filter); has_this_work_been_done (true/false); why_do_we_believe (chain, missing); what_context_packs_are_stale (stale/clean pack); what_world_models_conflict (with/without graph); what_assumptions_underlie (with/without graph); what_generated_artifacts_depend_on (with/without graph); what_confidence_collapsed (low-hyp, active excluded); what_can_agent_do_next (unblocked, no graph); what_should_agent_not_do (stop_condition, empty store); what_skills_apply (match, no match); what_state_delta_would_complete (with/without graph); is_this_action_duplicate (true/false); what_context_pack_minimizes_tokens (list, no invalid).
- **Requirement ID:** REQ-CM-018
- **Type:** unit
- **Verification Method:** evaluator
- **Confidence:** 1.0

## TEST-CM-019. Token metrics CRUD and efficiency report
- **ID:** TEST-CM-019
- **Title:** Token metrics CRUD and efficiency report
- **Description:** 5 token-metrics tests: record_token_metric writes kind=token_metric WAL record with correct task_id and total_tokens; get_token_metrics filters by task_id; token_efficiency_report returns zeros for empty store; efficiency report calculates tokens_per_success, avg_tool_calls, duplicate_block_rate correctly across mixed success/failure tasks; ChronoStore.record_token_metric convenience method works end-to-end.
- **Requirement ID:** REQ-CM-019
- **Type:** unit
- **Verification Method:** evaluator
- **Confidence:** 1.0

## TEST-CM-020. Skill system find and run
- **ID:** TEST-CM-020
- **Title:** Skill system find and run
- **Description:** 5 skill-system tests: find_skills matches by activation keyword intersection; find_skills returns [] on no match; record_skill_run writes kind=skill_run WAL record with correct skill_id and success=True at confidence=1.0; failed skill run writes confidence=0.5; ChronoStore.find_skills convenience method works; chain_valid() True after token metrics + skill runs.
- **Requirement ID:** REQ-CM-020
- **Type:** unit
- **Verification Method:** evaluator
- **Confidence:** 1.0

## TEST-CM-021. ChronoStore Phase 2 convenience methods
- **ID:** TEST-CM-021
- **Title:** ChronoStore Phase 2 convenience methods
- **Description:** Covered by test_rollback.py::test_store_invalidate_method, test_metrics.py::test_record_token_metric_store_method, test_metrics.py::test_store_find_skills_method, and test_metrics.py::test_chain_valid_after_metrics. All six ChronoStore Phase 2 convenience methods (invalidate, record_token_metric, get_token_metrics, token_efficiency_report, find_skills, record_skill_run) must delegate correctly to their module functions and produce identical results.
- **Requirement ID:** REQ-CM-021
- **Type:** unit
- **Verification Method:** evaluator
- **Confidence:** 1.0
